structured.description: An autonomous vehicle simulation environment using PySOTFW. It creates a graph that represents the environment, including obstacles and landmarks, and updates it in real-time based on user input. The code also implements a DSR (Dense Slammed Reconstruction) algorithm to estimate the pose of the vehicle in the environment. Additionally, it provides tools for visualizing the simulation and updating node and edge attributes.

items.description: Is a part of a larger system for real-time SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) in a robotic environment. It provides methods for updating the graph, computing the covariance matrix, and visualizing the g2o graph. Additionally, it handles the startup check, update edge attribute names, update edge set, delete edge set, and delete node set.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: Used to set the interval between successive calls to the `startup_check` function, which is responsible for checking if the application is still running after a certain period of time.

attributes.name: agent_id
attributes.description: Used to store the ID of the agent that the worker represents.

attributes.name: g
attributes.description: Used to represent the graph structure of the environment, which is essential for the worker's tasks such as edge measurement and room initialization.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: Used to check if the main thread is still running after a certain period of time (200 milliseconds) and quit the application if it is.

attributes.name: rt_api
attributes.description: Used to specify the API for the RT (Real-Time) module of the worker, which controls the interaction between the worker and the robot's RT system.

attributes.name: inner_api
attributes.description: A reference to the inner API of the worker's robot, allowing access to its methods and attributes for communication with other components of the system.

attributes.name: odometry_node_id
attributes.description: 0-based indexed, which represents the node ID of the odometry node in the graph. It's used to identify the node in the graph that corresponds to the robot's current pose.

attributes.name: odometry_queue
attributes.description: Used to store odometry data points from the ROS bag file, which are used to compute the nominal corners of the room.

attributes.name: last_odometry
attributes.description: Used to store the last known odometry of the robot, which is used to compute the covariance matrix of the robot's pose. It is updated every time a new odometry measurement is received from the robot.

attributes.name: g2o
attributes.description: Used to configure the G2O optimization algorithm for real-time SLAM. It enables the worker to interact with the G2O optimizer and perform operations such as setting the initial guess, adding edges, and updating node attributes.

attributes.name: visualizer
attributes.description: A reference to an instance of the `Visualizer` class, which is used for visualizing the graph and its evolution during the optimization process.

attributes.name: odometry_noise_std_dev
attributes.description: 1.0 by default, representing the standard deviation of the noise in the odometry measurements. It is used to scale the odometry readings for better convergence of the optimization algorithm.

attributes.name: odometry_noise_angle_std_dev
attributes.description: Representing the standard deviation of the angle noise in the odometry measurements. It affects how much the robot's pose is likely to deviate from its intended path due to noisy angle measurements.

attributes.name: measurement_noise_std_dev
attributes.description: Used to represent the standard deviation of measurement noise in the robot's odometry readings. It helps to quantify the uncertainty in the robot's position and orientation estimates.

attributes.name: last_room_id
attributes.description: Used to keep track of the room ID that was last processed by the worker before switching rooms. It is updated when the worker moves to a new room, so it can be used to determine which room the worker is currently processing.

attributes.name: actual_room_id
attributes.description: Used to keep track of the current room ID during a navigation task, updating it whenever the worker moves to a new room through its `update_node` method.

attributes.name: elapsed
attributes.description: Updated every time a task is processed by the worker, indicating the elapsed time since the worker was created or since the last task was processed.

attributes.name: room_initialized
attributes.description: Set to False when the room ID changes, indicating that the worker has not yet initialized the new room. It is updated to True after the worker initializes the new room.

attributes.name: iterations
attributes.description: 0 by default. It represents the number of iterations to perform during the optimization process. Each iteration consists of solving the optimization problem for a subset of the edges in the graph, and the value of this attribute determines how many times the optimization algorithm will execute. A higher value of `iterations` can lead to a more accurate solution, but also increases the computational time required for the optimization process.

attributes.name: hide
attributes.description: Used to hide or show specific parts of the worker's functionality, such as the `visualize_g2o_realtime`, `update_node_att`, and `delete_edge` methods.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: Used to schedule a call to the `startup_check` function every 200 milliseconds to check if the application should quit.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: Used to compute the marginals of the graph, which are then used for pose estimation.

attributes.name: init_graph
attributes.description:
Utilized to track whether the graph has been initialized or not by the worker. It is set to True when the
worker finishes initializing the graph and False when it has not. This feature helps ensure that
the robot's actions are taken in light of the most recent information about its environment

attributes.name: current_edge_set
attributes.description: Used to keep track of whether the edge set has been updated with the latest room information. It is initialized as True initially and is updated to False when the edge set is updated with new room information, allowing the worker to stop processing edges and move on to other tasks.

attributes.name: first_rt_set
attributes.description: Set to `True` when the first RT translation, rotation, or both are received from the G2O graph. It is used to keep track of whether the RT data has been received for the first time.

attributes.name: translation_to_set
attributes.description: Used to store the translation of a specific RT edge set from a reference frame to a target frame. It represents the difference in position between the two frames.

attributes.name: rotation_to_set
attributes.description: Used to store the rotation of the edge from its original position to the desired position for g2o optimization.

attributes.name: room_polygon
attributes.description: Used to store a list of vertices that represent the boundary of a virtual room in the robot's workspace, as perceived by the worker's sensor. The vertices are represented by (x, y) coordinates in the workspace.

attributes.name: initialize_g2o_graph
attributes.description: Used to initialize a graph structure with the g2o library, which is used for visualizing and optimizing the robot's path. It takes no arguments and returns no value.

attributes.name: update_node_att
attributes.description: Used to update the attributes of a node in the graph when it's a room node. It takes as arguments the node ID, the attribute names, and the new values for each attribute.

attributes.name: update_edge
attributes.description: Used to update the edge attributes based on the ROS messages received by the worker. It handles updates for current, RT, and nominal edges and sets the translation and rotation towers to their corresponding values in the ROS message.

attributes.name: update_edge_att
attributes.description: Used to update the attributes of an edge in a graph based on its type (current or RT). It takes the edge's ID, type, and attribute names as inputs and updates the corresponding attributes in the graph.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Initializes an object of the SpecificWorker class, setting up various components such as graphs, nodes, edges, and timers. It also sets properties like period, odometry noise, and room ID.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Passed to the superclass's constructor, indicating that the SpecificWorker class will use this map as its proxy map for accessing the graph.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the G2O graph has already been initialized or not.

items.name: __init__

items.description: In the `SpecificWorker` class processes robot odometry data and updates the graph using G2O optimization. It adds landmarks to the graph based on the robot's movement and computes the RT edges between nodes.
items.name: compute

items.description: Initializes a G2O graph for a specific room based on the robot's odometry and RoomTune API information. It adds nominal corners to the graph and calculates the room polygon.

returns.description: 1 if the g2o graph could be initialized successfully, and 0 otherwise.

items.name: initialize_g2o_graph

items.description: Calculates the displacement of an object based on its odometry data, taking into account acceleration, lateral movement, and angular velocity.

params.name: odometry
params.description: An enumeration of odometry values recorded by the agent over time.

returns.description: The lateral displacement (in meters), the advance displacement(in meters) and the angular displacement (in radians).

items.name: get_displacement

items.description: Computes the covariance matrix of a set of vertices in a graph using the G2O optimizer. It returns the computed covariance matrix and an indication of whether the computation was successful.

params.name: vertex
params.description: Used to compute the covariance matrix for a specific vertex in the graph.

returns.description: A tuple containing two elements: (1) a boolean value indicating whether the covariance matrix was computed successfully, and (2) the covariance matrix itself.

items.name: get_covariance_matrix

items.description: Within the SpecificWorker class loads G2O data from an archivo, visualizes the vertices and edges in a 3D scatter plot with colors and markers, and updates the plot in real-time as new data is loaded.

params.name: optimizer
params.description: Used to load a G2O file and estimate its vertices positions in real-time.

items.name: visualize_g2o_realtime

items.description: Updates the attributes of a node in a graph, specifically the odometry node, by appending a new value to an internal queue for further processing.

params.name: id
params.description: Used to represent the unique identifier of the node being updated.

params.name: attribute_names
params.description: An array of strings that represents the names of attributes to be updated on the node.

items.name: update_node_att

items.description: Updates a node with ID `id` based on its type, performing different actions depending on the type.

params.name: id
params.description: Passed as an argument to update the node with the given id.

params.name: type
params.description: Passed as "corner"

items.name: update_node

items.description: In the `SpecificWorker` class deletes a node from the graph represented by the class.

params.name: id
params.description: Passed as an argument to delete a node from the tree.

items.name: delete_node

items.description: Updates the current room ID and translation/rotation to set for an RT agent based on the received edge message. It also sets the `room_initialized` flag and prints debug messages.

params.name: fr
params.description: Representative of an internal node index. It plays a role in identifying the particular edge being updated.

params.name: to
params.description: A target node index indicating the destination node for the edge to be updated.

params.name: type
params.description: Either "current" or "RT". It indicates the type of edge to be updated.

items.name: update_edge

items.description: Deletes an edge from a graph represented as an adjacency list, identified by its source and destination vertices and the edge type.

params.name: fr
params.description: Used as the first index of the edge to be deleted.

params.name: to
params.description:
The index of the edge to be deleted after it has been selected from the range 
[fr, to).

params.name: type
params.description: Used to specify the type of edge being deleted, with possible values 'in' or 'out'.

items.name: delete_edge

structured.description: A DSR (Decision Support System for Robotics) environment for a robot that can move around in a room and interact with objects inside it. The code includes various functions and methods for updating the robot's state, inserting edges into the graph representing the room, and deleting edges when necessary. It also includes startup checks to ensure the robot is in the correct state before starting the DSR algorithm. Additionally, there are functions for generating a room picture based on the robot's current position and inserting a current edge into the graph if the robot is not already in a room.

items.description: Manages and updates a specific agent's internal graph, handling various events such as node and edge creation, deletion, and attribute changes. It also generates room images and performs startup checks.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 200, indicating that the worker will take a break every 200 seconds (3 minutes) before continuing to work.

attributes.name: agent_id
attributes.description: Used to identify the agent that owns the graph. It is used to determine which edges are current edges and which nodes to update when updating node attributes or adding new nodes and edges.

attributes.name: g
attributes.description: Used to represent the graph of the environment. It stores all the nodes, edges, and attributes of the environment, and is used for traversing, searching, and manipulating the environment.

attributes.name: update_node
attributes.description: Used to update the state of a node based on its type, such as "completed" or "crossed". It takes two arguments: `id` (int) and `type` (str), which represent the ID of the node and the type of update respectively.

attributes.name: update_edge
attributes.description: Used to update the edge attributes based on the type of edge and the specified attribute names. It updates the edge attributes by setting the `type` field of the Edge object to the specified type and updating the `attribute_names` field with the desired attribute names.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: Used to start the quit process after 200 milliseconds of starting the worker.

attributes.name: rt_api
attributes.description: Used to specify the API for real-time updates on the worker's graph.

attributes.name: inner_api
attributes.description: Used to access the inner API of the agent's environment, which allows for retrieving information about the current room, doors, and other nodes in the graph.

attributes.name: room_initialized
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the room has not been initialized yet. It is used to keep track of whether a room has been initialized or not.

attributes.name: robot_name
attributes.description: Used to store the name of the robot that the worker is controlling.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to represent the ID of the robot that the worker is associated with.

attributes.name: state
attributes.description: Used to store the current state of the worker (either "idle", "running", or "crossed").

attributes.name: affordance_node_active_id
attributes.description: Used to store the ID of the node representing the affordance of the robot, which indicates whether the robot is active or not.

attributes.name: room_exit_door_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, representing the ID of the door through which the agent exits a room. It's used to identify the current room in the agent's internal graph when no other information is available.

attributes.name: exit_room_node_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, representing the ID of the room node that the agent exits when it reaches the end of a room.

attributes.name: enter_room_node_id
attributes.description: Used to store the ID of the room node that the worker enters when it completes its task.

attributes.name: exit_door_id
attributes.description: 0 by default, which represents the door that leads out of the current room in the agent's internal graph. It is used to determine when a new room should be created and the old one deleted.

attributes.name: graph
attributes.description: Used to represent the graph of a specific worker's environment, containing nodes and edges that represent the worker's location and movements within the environment.

attributes.name: vertex_size
attributes.description: 1 by default, which represents the size of each vertex in the graph. It can be adjusted to control the number of vertices in the graph.

attributes.name: not_required_attrs
attributes.description: Used to specify a list of attributes that are not required for the worker's functionality, but can still be useful for debugging or other purposes.

attributes.name: fig
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the worker does not have a figure.

attributes.name: ax
attributes.description: Used to store the affordance node's BT state, which indicates whether the node has completed its task or not.

attributes.name: insert_current_edge
attributes.description: Used to insert a new edge into the graph connecting the current room node with itself, indicating that the agent is currently in this room.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: Used to schedule a call to the `startup_check` method after 200 milliseconds of starting the worker.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: Used to compute the similarity between a room and the gists of other rooms in the agent's internal graph, adjusting door connections accordingly.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Sets up various components and connections for an instance of the `SpecificWorker` class, including creating a graph, setting properties and timers, and connecting to signals for updates and removals.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to map the original graph to a proxy graph, which allows for efficient computation of graph properties without affecting the original graph.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the agent has already started up, avoiding unnecessary startup checks when the agent has already run.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Sets parameters for a specific worker instance, removes a self-edge from a room, stores ID of an exit door, and adds attributes to both doors.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to set parameters for the function's execution.

items.name: setParams

items.description: Determines the current state of a worker based on its graph and updates the state accordingly.
items.name: compute

items.description: Determines if there is a room exit door id and sets the state to "crossing" if there is only one current room edge.
items.name: idle

items.description: Performs actions when the worker reaches a certain state, including printing messages and setting the worker's state to "crossed".
items.name: crossing

items.description: Within the `SpecificWorker` class updates the state of the worker based on the value of an edge attribute and performs actions depending on the current state.
items.name: crossed

items.description: Searches for nodes with certain names and IDs within the graph, then sets its enter room node ID to the first matching node's ID and inserts a current edge to it. Finally, it updates its state to "initializing doors."
items.name: initializing_room

items.description:
In the `SpecificWorker` class:

1. Finds the other side door node in the graph.
2. Creates a new edge and vertex in the graph to represent the robot's movement through the door.
3. Updates the robot's parent attribute with the room ID of the other side door node.
items.name: known_room

items.description: Determines the connected rooms of an agent, based on its exit door ID, and sets the other side door ID, room name, and connected room name attributes of the exit door node and its adjacent nodes in the graph.
items.name: initializing_doors

items.description: Is part of the `SpecificWorker` class, which inherits from `GenericWorker`. The function stores the graph represented by an igraph object in the `self.g` variable, and then tries to find a node in the graph with the specified `room_exit_door_id` using `vs.find()`. If the node is found, the function prints a message and returns. If the node is not found, the function inserts a new room node into the graph using `traverse_graph()` and then prints a message before drawing the updated graph using `draw_graph()`. Finally, the function sets its state to "removing".
items.name: store_graph

items.description: Removes RT and has edges that lead to a specific room (room number determined by `room_exit_door_id`) and deletes the corresponding nodes in the graph. It also updates a dictionary of old room numbers for each node in the graph.
items.name: removing

items.description: Within `SpecificWorker` class recursively traverses a directed graph represented by an IGraph object, starting from a given node ID. It inserts the given node and its RT children into the graph, then traverses the children's destinations.

params.name: node_id
params.description: A unique identifier of a node in the graph.

items.name: traverse_graph

items.description: Recursively traverses the graph, inserting vertices and edges into a data structure called DSR for each vertex with a higher level than the current node.

params.name: node
params.description: Passed as a reference to a vertex object representing a specific node in the graph.

items.name: traverse_igraph

items.description: Adds a new vertex to an igraph object, passing it the necessary attributes and actions based on their types.

params.name: node
params.description: Passed as an instance of this class.

items.name: insert_igraph_vertex

items.description: Within the SpecificWorker class modifies an input node and adds it to the graph. It sets the new node's parent attribute to the id of the specified parent node, and then iterates through the node's attributes, adding each non-optional attribute with its value from the input node. Finally, the method calls `insert_node` on the graph with the new node.

params.name: parent_name
params.description: Used to specify the name of the parent node for the new vertex being inserted.

params.name: node
params.description: Passed with the function call, representing an existing vertex in the graph to be inserted as a child of another existing vertex.

items.name: insert_dsr_vertex

items.description: Adds an edge to an igraph representation of a graph, providing the origin and destination nodes and their attribute values for rotation and translation.

params.name: edge
params.description: Used to insert an edge into an igraph graph.

items.name: insert_igraph_edge

items.description: Adds an edge to the graph with RT and rotation attributes, based on input org and dest nodes.

params.name: org
params.description: Used to represent the origin node of the DSR edge being inserted.

params.name: dest
params.description: Represented by an object containing name, id, and other attributes.

items.name: insert_dsr_edge

items.description: Generates and plots a graph based on a Kamada-Kawai layout, with nodes labeled and edges highlighted with arrows.
items.name: draw_graph

items.description: Determines the room number associated with a given node ID in a graph, retrieving the value from the node's attribute "room_id" or returning -1 if an exception occurs during the attempt.

params.name: node_id
params.description: Used to retrieve a specific node from the graph.

returns.description: The room ID associated with a given node ID.

items.name: check_element_room_number

items.description: Within the SpecificWorker class checks for an element level attribute in a given node and returns its value if found, or -1 otherwise.

params.name: node_id
params.description: Represented by an identifier for a specific node in a graph.

items.name: check_element_level

items.description: Generates an image of a room by traversing the room's nodes and edges in a graph, and retrieving attributes such as the room ID and translation information. It then draws the room polygon and doors, and saves the image to a file.

params.name: room_node_id
params.description: Used to retrieve the node object associated with a specific room id.

items.name: generate_room_picture

items.description: Within the SpecificWorker class updates the current edge for the agent by inserting or assigning an edge in the graph based on the given room ID.

params.name: room_id
params.description: Passed as the ID of the current room where the agent is located.

items.name: insert_current_edge

items.description: Updates the state of an affordance node based on its ID and type. If the active ID matches the node ID, it prints out information about the node's state. If the state is completed and not active, it sets the state to "crossed".

params.name: id
params.description: A unique identifier for each node in the scene graph, used to identify the node being updated.

params.name: type
params.description: Used to identify the type of affordance node being updated, which helps determine the appropriate actions to take based on the node's state.

items.name: update_node

items.description: Updates an edge in the graph when a specific condition is met, involving the node's robot ID, type, and the presence of an edge.

params.name: fr
params.description: Representing the source node of the edge being updated.

params.name: to
params.description: Used to represent the target node ID for the update operation.

params.name: type
params.description: Used to indicate the type of edge being updated, specifically "RT".

items.name: update_edge
